scope.summary:
Goal: Implement VS Code parity 'Format Document' (Alt+Shift+F) and 'Format Selection' for the native egui code editor. Both actions request edits from the language server over the existing stdio LSP client (textDocument/formatting for the whole document, textDocument/rangeFormatting for the active selection), then apply the returned LSP TextEdit array to the ropey buffer as a SINGLE undo step so one Ctrl+Z reverts the entire format. This wires three existing surfaces created by earlier MTs: the EDIT menu 'Format Document' entry, the Editor Body Context Menu 'Format Selection' and 'Format Document' entries, and the MT-010 keymap (Alt+Shift+F). When no language server is attached for the buffer's language, the actions are disabled (greyed) with a hover tooltip 'No formatter available for this language'. The expected diff shape is: (a) one new Rust module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/formatting.rs (~250-400 lines) holding the request builders, the LSP-TextEdit-to-buffer applier, the single-undo grouping, and the no-LSP capability check; (b) edits to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs adding the two typed request methods (format_document, format_range) and their response deserialization; (c) edits to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs adding two new CodeEditorAction enum variants (FormatDocument, FormatSelection) with the Alt+Shift+F default binding for FormatDocument.

Exact files to CREATE:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/formatting.rs — public surface: `pub fn format_document(editor: &mut CodeEditor, lsp: &mut LspClient) -> FormatOutcome`, `pub fn format_selection(editor: &mut CodeEditor, lsp: &mut LspClient) -> FormatOutcome`, `pub fn formatter_available(lsp: &LspClient, language_id: &str) -> bool`, and `fn apply_text_edits(buffer: &mut TextBuffer, edits: &[LspTextEdit], undo: &mut UndoStack) -> usize`. `FormatOutcome` is an enum: `Applied { edit_count: usize }` | `NoChange` | `NoFormatter` | `LspError(String)`.

Exact files to EDIT:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs — add `pub fn format_document(&mut self, uri: &str, options: FormattingOptions) -> Result<Vec<LspTextEdit>, LspError>` issuing a 'textDocument/formatting' request, and `pub fn format_range(&mut self, uri: &str, range: LspRange, options: FormattingOptions) -> Result<Vec<LspTextEdit>, LspError>` issuing a 'textDocument/rangeFormatting' request. Add serde structs `FormattingOptions { tab_size: u32, insert_spaces: bool }`, `LspTextEdit { range: LspRange, new_text: String }`, `LspRange { start: LspPosition, end: LspPosition }`, `LspPosition { line: u32, character: u32 }` if not already present from MT-008. Read the server's documentFormattingProvider / documentRangeFormattingProvider capability flags from the initialize result so `formatter_available` can consult them.
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs — add `FormatDocument` and `FormatSelection` to the `CodeEditorAction` enum; in the default keymap table bind `FormatDocument` to KeyboardShortcut { modifiers: ALT | SHIFT, key: F }. `FormatSelection` has no default binding (menu/context-menu invoked) unless the React reference defines one. Ensure the keymap dispatch routes these actions to the formatting.rs entry points.
- (MENU WIRING) The EDIT menu and Editor Body Context Menu builders (created in earlier E1 menu MTs) must call `format_document` / `format_selection`; locate the existing menu builder where the EDIT menu items are constructed (the EDIT menu lives in the WP-011 top_menu_bar.rs surface; the editor body context menu reuses the WP-011 context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs surfaces — search for the EDIT menu label / existing 'Undo'/'Redo' entries) and add 'Format Document' to the EDIT menu and 'Format Selection' + 'Format Document' to the editor body context menu. Each menu item must reflect enabled/disabled state from `formatter_available`. If the menu builder file is outside the allowed_paths of this MT, do the minimal wiring inside formatting.rs by exposing a `menu_descriptors()` helper that the menu builder already consumes; do NOT modify menu files not listed in allowed_paths — instead expose the helper and record a typed blocker if the menu builder cannot consume it.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. FormattingOptions: derive tab_size and insert_spaces from the editor's current indent settings (default tab_size=4, insert_spaces=true if the editor has no per-language override). Do NOT hardcode; read from the editor config struct established in MT-001/MT-010.
  2. format_document flow: (i) check formatter_available; if false return FormatOutcome::NoFormatter. (ii) build the document URI (file:// path the buffer was opened from; if untitled, use the in-memory uri scheme already used by MT-008's LSP didOpen). (iii) call lsp.format_document(uri, options). (iv) on Ok(edits): apply_text_edits as one undo group; return Applied { edit_count } or NoChange if edits is empty. (v) on Err: return LspError(msg) and surface a non-blocking toast; never panic.
  3. format_selection flow: identical but compute LspRange from the editor's current primary selection (start..end). If selection is empty/collapsed, fall back to formatting the whole document is WRONG — instead VS Code formats the current line/empty selection per server behavior; keep it simple: if selection is empty, send the cursor line's range (start of line .. end of line). Convert buffer byte offsets to LSP line/character positions using the TextBuffer byte_to_line + UTF-16 column math (LSP positions are UTF-16 code units by default; convert via the buffer's char/utf16 helpers — do NOT assume bytes == columns).
  4. apply_text_edits MUST sort the incoming TextEdits in descending order of start position before applying, so earlier edits do not invalidate the offsets of later ones. Open ONE undo transaction (UndoStack::begin_group / end_group, or the equivalent batch API from MT-010), apply every edit inside it, then commit so a single Ctrl+Z reverts all. Return the number of edits applied. Re-establish a sensible cursor/selection after formatting (clamp old cursor to new buffer length).
  5. formatter_available: return true only if an LSP client is attached for the buffer's language_id AND the server advertised documentFormattingProvider (for format_document) / documentRangeFormattingProvider (for format_selection) in its capabilities. If no LSP at all, return false -> menu/keymap action disabled.
  6. No-LSP path: the EDIT menu item and context-menu items render disabled (egui add_enabled(false, ...)) with hover tooltip 'No formatter available for this language'. The keymap Alt+Shift+F, when fired with no formatter, must be a no-op (optionally a brief status-bar note) and must NOT panic or block the frame.
  7. AccessKit: the menu items and context-menu items already carry AccessKit metadata from their owning MTs; for any new enabled/disabled state, ensure the disabled state is reflected in the AccessKit node (set the node as disabled / not focusable) so swarm agents observe the same gating the human sees.

React reference: app/src/lib/monaco/* — Monaco wired Format Document via editor.getAction('editor.action.formatDocument').run() and Format Selection via 'editor.action.formatSelection'; Monaco handled the LSP round-trip internally. Here we replace Monaco's internal action with an explicit LSP textDocument/formatting + rangeFormatting call and an explicit single-undo TextEdit applier. Port the action IDs, the EDIT-menu / context-menu placement, and the disabled-when-no-formatter UX from the Monaco wiring; do NOT port Monaco runtime code.

Backend binding: bind the EXISTING stdio LSP transport stood up in MT-008 (textDocument/formatting, textDocument/rangeFormatting). No backend rewrite. If MT-008's LspClient does not yet expose a generic request/response round-trip these methods can build on, record a typed blocker (do not rewrite the transport). PostgreSQL/EventLedger only for any persisted state; this MT persists nothing new (formatting is buffer-local), so no datastore work is expected — if any persistence is implied, it MUST go through the EventLedger/Postgres path, never SQLite.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-008 (LSP client + stdio transport, didOpen URI scheme, capabilities parsing) must be merged first — this MT binds its request plumbing.
  - MT-010 (keymap + CodeEditorAction enum + UndoStack batch/group API) must be merged first — this MT adds two action variants and relies on single-undo grouping.

Risk if missed: the EDIT menu 'Format Document' entry and the editor body context-menu 'Format Selection' / 'Format Document' entries are dead (clickable but inert), and LSP formatting parity with VS Code is unmet, blocking MT-044.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: format_document applies the LSP-returned TextEdit array to the buffer as a SINGLE undo step — proven by a test that mocks the LSP client to return multiple TextEdits, calls format_document, asserts the buffer matches the expected formatted text, then asserts exactly ONE UndoStack entry was created and a single undo reverts the entire format.

AC-002: format_selection edits only the selected range — proven by a test that selects a sub-range, mocks rangeFormatting to return an edit, calls format_selection, and asserts text OUTSIDE the selected range is byte-for-byte unchanged.

AC-003: With no LSP attached (or server lacking documentFormattingProvider), formatter_available returns false, the EDIT menu and context-menu items render disabled, and firing Alt+Shift+F is a no-op that does not panic — proven by the format_no_lsp test.

AC-004: Alt+Shift+F is bound to CodeEditorAction::FormatDocument in the default keymap and routes to formatting.rs — proven by a keymap dispatch test and by an egui_kittest harness that presses Alt+Shift+F and observes the buffer reflow.

AC-005: TextEdits are applied in descending-start order so overlapping/sequential edits do not corrupt offsets — proven by a test feeding two non-trivial edits whose naive ascending application would mis-align, asserting the correct final text.
AC-006: cargo test -p handshake-native passes with no failures across all formatting tests; no unwrap()/panic on the LSP-error path (LspError(String) returned and surfaced as a non-blocking toast).

AC-007: LSP positions are converted using UTF-16 column semantics (not raw byte offsets), proven by a test with a non-ASCII line where byte offset != UTF-16 column and the applied edit lands at the correct character.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native format_document -- --nocapture  =>  mock LSP TextEdits applied, single undo entry created, all GREEN
PT-002: cargo test -p handshake-native format_selection -- --nocapture  =>  only the selected range edited, text outside unchanged, GREEN
PT-003: cargo test -p handshake-native format_no_lsp -- --nocapture  =>  action disabled, no panic, GREEN
PT-004: egui_kittest harness: press Alt+Shift+F, assert FormatDocument fires and the buffer reflows to the mocked formatted text
implementation_notes: Apply TextEdits sorted descending by start position; open one UndoStack group (MT-010 batch API) around the whole apply so a single Ctrl+Z reverts the entire format.
FormattingOptions { tab_size, insert_spaces } must read the editor's current indent config (MT-001/MT-010), defaulting tab_size=4 / insert_spaces=true; do not hardcode.
LSP positions are UTF-16 code units by default — convert buffer byte offsets via the TextBuffer char/utf16 helpers; never assume bytes == columns.

FormatOutcome enum (Applied { edit_count } | NoChange | NoFormatter | LspError(String)) keeps the no-formatter and error paths typed and panic-free; surface LspError as a non-blocking toast, not a frame-blocking dialog.

Disabled menu/context-menu items use egui add_enabled(false, ...) with hover tooltip 'No formatter available for this language' and must set the AccessKit node disabled so swarm agents observe the gating.
Empty/collapsed selection in format_selection sends the current line's range (line start .. line end), matching VS Code behavior; do NOT silently fall back to whole-document formatting.
Re-clamp cursor/selection to the new buffer length after applying edits so the caret does not point past end-of-buffer.
If MT-008's LspClient lacks a generic request round-trip to build format_document/format_range on, record a typed blocker — do NOT rewrite the stdio transport.

PATH + DISPATCH SCOPE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24): the contract's allowed_paths omit the DISPATCH site + test. The format actions are DEAD without panel.rs wiring (the keymap FormatDocument/FormatSelection dispatch -> formatting::format_document/selection with the CodeEditorPanel's TextBuffer + lsp). allowed_paths corrected: formatting.rs (new) + lsp_client.rs (additive format_document/format_range + capability flags, no 2nd transport) + keymap.rs (FormatDocument+Alt+Shift+F, FormatSelection) + panel.rs (the CodeEditorPanel dispatch arms - the real type is CodeEditorPanel NOT 'CodeEditor'; formatting.rs operates on it/TextBuffer) + mod.rs (pub mod) + tests/test_formatting.rs. EDIT-menu Format Document: top_menu_bar.rs is in allowed_paths for MINIMAL ADDITIVE wiring of the EDIT-menu 'Format Document' entry (reflecting formatter_available enabled/disabled); the editor-body context-menu entries route through the same path. If a needed menu file is genuinely not editable, expose menu_descriptors() from formatting.rs + typed blocker (no scope-bleed beyond allowed_paths, RISK-007).

DATA-INTEGRITY: SINGLE-UNDO + DESCENDING-OFFSET + UTF-16 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): apply the LSP TextEdit array in DESCENDING start-offset order (reuse MT-048's rename::apply_text_edits_to_buffer / the same descending applier - RISK-001/MC-004, AC-005: ascending corrupts offsets) wrapped in ONE undo GROUP so a single Ctrl+Z reverts the WHOLE format (RISK-002/MC-001, AC-001 - use the existing code-editor undo group/batch API; if MT-010's UndoStack lacks a group API, use the same single-transaction approach MT-048 used). LSP POSITIONS ARE UTF-16 CODE UNITS (RISK-003/MC-005, AC-007 - the load-bearing i18n correctness gate): convert buffer byte offsets <-> LSP line/character via the TextBuffer's char/utf16 helpers, NEVER assume bytes==columns; TEST a non-ASCII line where byte offset != UTF-16 column + assert the edit lands at the correct character. After apply, CLAMP cursor/selection to the new buffer length (RISK-006 - no out-of-range slice panic). format_selection: empty/collapsed selection -> the current LINE's range (line-start..line-end), NOT whole-doc (RISK per VS Code behavior); text OUTSIDE the selected range byte-for-byte unchanged (AC-002).

GATING + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): formatter_available = LSP attached for the buffer's language_id AND the server advertised documentFormattingProvider (format_document) / documentRangeFormattingProvider (format_selection) in initialize capabilities (RISK-004/MC-003). NO-FORMATTER: EDIT-menu + context-menu items render DISABLED (egui add_enabled(false,...)) with hover 'No formatter available for this language' + the AccessKit node set DISABLED (swarm observes the gating); Alt+Shift+F with no formatter is a NO-OP, no panic, no frame block (AC-003). FormatOutcome enum (Applied{edit_count}|NoChange|NoFormatter|LspError(String)) - NO unwrap()/panic on any path; LspError surfaced as a NON-BLOCKING toast (AC-006/MC-006). FormattingOptions{tab_size,insert_spaces} read from the editor's indent config (MT-001/010, default 4/true - do NOT hardcode). Off-thread LSP request (don't block the frame, RISK-005, MT-008 pattern). FULLY provable WITHOUT live PG (mock LSP returning TextEdits; in-memory buffer; the single-undo + descending + UTF-16 + no-formatter all standalone). Screenshot (if any) EXTERNAL not test_output/. Full suite green (use cargo test -j 2 - ~90 bins OOM at default -j); clippy clean; no SQLite (MC-007 - nothing persisted).
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/monaco/* — Format Document / Format Selection action wiring: action IDs (editor.action.formatDocument, editor.action.formatSelection), EDIT-menu and editor-body context-menu placement, and the disabled-when-no-formatter UX. Port the wiring/UX only; do NOT port Monaco runtime code (replaced by explicit LSP textDocument/formatting + rangeFormatting).
binds_backend_api: LSP textDocument/formatting (stdio) — via the existing MT-008 LspClient transport; whole-document format edits.
LSP textDocument/rangeFormatting (stdio) — via the existing MT-008 LspClient transport; selection-range format edits.
Bind the EXISTING MT-008 stdio LSP plumbing (the handshake-native code_editor LSP client module) only; no backend rewrite. Gaps are recorded as typed blockers.

risks: RISK-001: LSP TextEdits applied in document (ascending) order invalidate later edit offsets, corrupting the buffer. Mitigate by sorting descending by start position before applying.
RISK-002: Each TextEdit pushed as its own undo entry breaks the 'single Ctrl+Z reverts the format' parity requirement. Mitigate by wrapping the whole apply in one UndoStack group/transaction.
RISK-003: Byte-offset vs UTF-16-column mismatch mis-places edits on lines with non-ASCII characters. Mitigate by converting positions via the buffer's UTF-16 helpers and testing a non-ASCII case.
RISK-004: No-formatter path panics or fires a dead menu item. Mitigate by formatter_available gating + disabled menu items + no-op keymap path, all panic-free.

RISK-005: LSP request blocks the egui frame thread while the server formats a large document. Mitigate by issuing the request without blocking the UI frame (async/poll per MT-008 transport) and surfacing a brief in-progress state; never spin-block the render loop.

RISK-006: Cursor/selection points past end-of-buffer after the document shrinks during format, causing an out-of-range slice panic on the next frame. Mitigate by clamping cursor/selection to the new buffer length after apply.

RISK-007: Modifying a menu-builder file outside this MT's allowed_paths to wire the entries causes scope bleed. Mitigate by exposing a menu_descriptors()/menu helper from formatting.rs for the existing builder to consume, and record a typed blocker if it cannot.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Test that multiple mocked TextEdits produce exactly one UndoStack entry and a single undo reverts the entire format (enforces AC-001).
MC-002: Test that format_selection leaves text outside the selected range byte-for-byte unchanged (enforces AC-002).
MC-003: Test the no-LSP / no-documentFormattingProvider path: formatter_available=false, menu items disabled, Alt+Shift+F no-op, no panic (enforces AC-003).
MC-004: Test descending-order application with edits whose naive ascending order would mis-align (enforces AC-005).
MC-005: Test a non-ASCII line where byte offset != UTF-16 column and assert the edit lands at the correct character (enforces AC-007).
MC-006: The LSP-error path returns FormatOutcome::LspError(String) and surfaces a non-blocking toast — assert no unwrap()/panic in any formatting code path.
MC-007: Any persisted state (none expected) must route through EventLedger/PostgreSQL; assert no SQLite dependency is introduced by this MT.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real code_editor/panel.rs + MT-008 transport + MT-048 descending applier + MT-010 undo + MT-004 selection + UTF-16/i18n + ~90-bin OOM
corrections: allowed_paths += panel.rs (dispatch; real type CodeEditorPanel not 'CodeEditor') + mod.rs + test + top_menu_bar.rs (minimal EDIT-menu wiring); additive LSP (no 2nd transport)
DATA-INTEGRITY: descending-offset apply (reuse MT-048) in ONE undo group (single Ctrl+Z); UTF-16 column conversion not raw bytes (test non-ASCII); cursor clamp; empty-sel->current-line
formatter_available capability gating (disabled menu + no-op keymap + accesskit disabled, no panic); FormatOutcome typed, no unwrap, non-blocking toast; off-thread
provable without live PG (mock LSP + in-memory); external screenshot; full suite via -j 2 (OOM); no SQLite

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_f4ace272 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: 3d94155a feat MT-050 Format Document + Format Selection (LSP)
implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..007 + PT-001..004 PASS; 9 unit + 13 integration + regression (keymap 12, rename 6, code_actions 7, top_menu_bar 7) green via -j 2; clippy clean; document reflow (formatted fn main(){ let x=1; }) visually verified - VS Code format parity; screenshot EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.data_integrity_held: DESCENDING-offset apply (ascending regression proven NOT-equal, AC-005) + SINGLE undo via the REAL InteractionBus (local_undo_count==1, single undo reverts the whole format, AC-001); UTF-16 column conversion VERIFIED on a 4-byte emoji + 3-byte CJK kanji (col->byte lands correct, never mid-codepoint; sums ch.len_utf16(), AC-007 - the i18n-relevant correctness gate); cursor clamp; empty-selection->current-line (surrounding text byte-for-byte unchanged, AC-002).
implementation_result.steers_held: panel.rs dispatch (real type CodeEditorPanel) + top_menu_bar.rs EDIT-menu entry; additive format_document/format_range on the MT-008 transport (no 2nd transport); formatter_available capability gating (no-LSP disabled menu + no-op keymap + AccessKit node disabled + toast, no panic); FormatOutcome typed (Applied/NoChange/NoFormatter/LspError) NO unwrap, non-blocking toast; off-thread arm-then-pump; Alt+Shift+F bind (keymap 60 variants).
implementation_result.honest_notes: (1) apply_text_edits signature deviation (no undo param) - MT-010's undo is a snapshot ring with no begin/end_group; contract's notes sanctioned the MT-048 single-transaction approach; single-undo proven against the REAL bus. (2) EDIT-menu 'Format Document' is a PRESENT-BUT-DISABLED placeholder until E11/MT-069 mounts the editor (consistent - lighting up the editor menu entries IS MT-069's job); the LIVE Alt+Shift+F path is fully proven.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: code_editor/formatting.rs (new) + lsp_client.rs (additive) + keymap.rs + panel.rs + mod.rs + top_menu_bar.rs (EDIT-menu entry) + test in allowed_paths.
